creator: Jasmine Hyare
source_text:
These Cinnamon Roll Cheesecake Protein Bites  taste like the center of a cinnamon roll, but they’re packed with protein and come together with just a handful of simple ingredients. Each bite is only 82 calories with 7g of protein, making them the perfect little snack or healthier dessert. 

Save this recipe and follow @healthysweetsandeats for more easy, high-protein recipes!

Macros (per bite, makes 10): 82 calories | 7g protein | 3.5g carbs | 5.4g fat | 1.5g fiber

Ingredients: 1/2 cup nonfat plain Greek yogurt 2 tbsp light cream cheese, softened
1/2 teaspoon maple extract
1/2 teaspoon vanilla bean paste (or vanilla extract) 1 scoop vanilla protein powder 3/4 cup finely ground almond flour 1 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon 1/4 tsp salt
Cinnamon Swirl: 1 tsp melted butter 1 tbsp coconut sugar 1 tsp ground cinnamon
Optional: 1 tablespoon of cane sugar, and 1 teaspoon of cinnamon for coating. 

Directions:
1️⃣ In a medium bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt and softened cream cheese until smooth (if your cream cheese is not soft enough, you may need to use a blender) Stir in the vanilla, maple, cinnamon, salt, and protein powder until well combined. Fold in the almond flour until a soft dough forms. Refrigerate for at least 20-30 minutes.

2️⃣ In a small bowl, mix together the melted butter, coconut sugar, and cinnamon. Gently fold the cinnamon mixture into the dough just 2-3 times so you create ribbons of cinnamon throughout without fully mixing it in.

3️⃣ Scoop into 10 equal portions, roll into balls, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour until firm. Store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freeze for a firmer, ice cream-like texture.

#glutenfree #cinnamonroll #proteinballs #easysnacks

notes: Agent-labeled from the frozen Instagram description before viewing parser output.
Unicode line separators delimit several ingredient lines; the main, cinnamon-swirl, and optional components form one ordered list.
Storage text remains part of the third numbered direction because it is authored within that same step.
